citations.description: Ticket status is `in_progress`; ticket comments contain only a FORCE_CLAIM bypass note, no workflow run ID, no captured ticket id, no `rebar verify-identity` output.
The signing key materialization step is present, but there is no evidence in the repo of an actual run having executed and produced a signed, verified ticket.

findings.criterion: A canary run (triggered via `workflow_dispatch` on `reconcile-bridge-canary.yml`) creates a bug/comment/transition whose event is signed as Rebar Bot: capture the created ticket id from the run log and confirm `rebar verify-authorship` (or `rebar verify-identity`) reports it `verified` and attributed to identity `594c-9dcf-5ad6-4e6d`.
findings.detail: This criterion requires evidence of a live workflow execution: a captured ticket id from the run log and a `rebar verify-authorship`/`verify-identity` report showing `verified` + identity `594c-9dcf-5ad6-4e6d`. The criterion is untagged (codebase-verifiable). No run log, captured ticket id, or verification output is present anywhere in the repository or ticket comments. The ticket shows status `in_progress` with no attestation of a completed workflow run. The codebase only contains the YAML configuration for the signing key injection; no artifact of an actual signed canary run exists in the repo.
findings.remediation: Trigger `workflow_dispatch` on `reconcile-bridge-canary.yml`, capture the created ticket id from the Actions run log, run `rebar verify-authorship <ticket-id>` (or `rebar verify-identity --all`) and record the output showing `verified` + identity `594c-9dcf-5ad6-4e6d` as a ticket comment with the Actions run URL and timestamp.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
